body:
﻿5014-C (Page 1 of 3)

Protected B when completed

2025

Form NU428

Nunavut Tax

For more information about this form, go to canada.ca/nu-tax-info.

Part A - Nunavut tax on taxable income

Line 1: Enter your taxable income from line 26000 of your return. ^

Use the amount from line 1 to complete the appropriate column below.

*** Transcriber's Note: Use column 1 if the amount from line 1 is $54,707 or
less. Use column 2 if the amount from line 1 is more than $54,707 but not
more than $109,413. Use column 3 if the amount from line 1 is more than
$109,413 but not more than $177,881. Use column 4 if the amount from line 1
is more than $177,881. ***

Column 1: Line 1 is $54,707 or less
Line 2: Amount from line 1 ^
Line 3: 0.00
Line 4: Line 2 minus line 3 (cannot be negative) = ^
Line 5: Rate 4%
Line 6: Line 4 multiplied by the percentage from line 5 = ^
Line 7: 0.00
Line 8: Line 6 plus line 7 Nunavut tax on taxable income = ^

Column 2: Line 1 is more than $54,707 but not more than $109,413
Line 2: Amount from line 1 ^
Line 3: 54,707.00
Line 4: Line 2 minus line 3 (cannot be negative) = ^
Line 5: Rate 7%
Line 6: Line 4 multiplied by the percentage from line 5 = ^
Line 7: 2,188.28
Line 8: Line 6 plus line 7 Nunavut tax on taxable income = ^

Column 3: Line 1 is more than $109,413 but not more than $177,881
Line 2: Amount from line 1 ^
Line 3: 109,413.00
Line 4: Line 2 minus line 3 (cannot be negative) = ^
Line 5: Rate 9%
Line 6: Line 4 multiplied by the percentage from line 5 = ^
Line 7: 6,017.70
Line 8: Line 6 plus line 7 Nunavut tax on taxable income = ^

Column 4: Line 1 is more than $177,881
Line 2: Amount from line 1 ^
Line 3: 177,881.00
Line 4: Line 2 minus line 3 (cannot be negative) = ^
Line 5: Rate 11.5%
Line 6: Line 4 multiplied by the percentage from line 5 = ^
Line 7: 12,179.82
Line 8: Line 6 plus line 7 Nunavut tax on taxable income = ^

Enter the amount from line 8 on line 53 and continue at line 9.

Part B - Nunavut non-refundable tax credits

Line 56140: Internal use

Line 9 and line 58040: Basic personal amount Claim $19,274 ^

Line 10 and line 58080: Age amount (if you were born in 1960 or earlier) (use
Worksheet NU428) (maximum $12,303) ^

Spouse or common-law partner amount:
Line 11: Base amount 19,274.00

Line 12: Your spouse's or common-law partner's net income from line 23600 of
their return ^

Line 13 and line 58120: Line 11 minus line 12 (if negative, enter "0") = ^

Amount for an eligible dependant:
Line 14: Base amount 19,274.00

Line 15: Your eligible dependant's net income from line 23600 of their return
^

Line 16 and line 58160: Line 14 minus line 15 (if negative, enter "0") = ^

Line 17 and line 58200: Amount for infirm dependants age 18 or older (use
Worksheet NU428) ^

Line 18 and line 58230: Amount for young children less than 6 years of age
(complete the chart on the last page) Line 63710: Number of children ^
multiplied by $1,200 = ^

Line 19: Add lines 9, 10, 13, and 16 to 18. = ^

CPP or QPP contributions:
Line 20 and line 58240: Amount from line 30800 of your return ^

Line 21 and line 58280: Amount from line 31000 of your return ^

Employment insurance premiums:
Line 22 and line 58300: Amount from line 31200 of your return ^

Line 23 and line 58305: Amount from line 31217 of your return ^

Line 24: Add lines 20 to 23. = ^

Line 25: Line 19 plus line 24 = ^

5014-C (Page 2 of 3)

Part B - Nunavut non-refundable tax credits (continued)

Line 26: Amount from line 25 of the previous page ^

Line 27 and line 58360: Pension income amount (amount from line 31400 of your
return) (maximum $2,000) ^

Line 28 and line 58400: Caregiver amount (use Worksheet NU428) ^

Line 29: Add lines 26 to 28. = ^

Line 30 and line 58440: Disability amount for self (claim $16,405 or, if you
were under 18 years of age, use Worksheet NU428) ^

Line 31 and line 58480: Disability amount transferred from a dependant (use
Worksheet NU428) ^

Line 32: Add lines 29 to 31. = ^

Line 33 and line 58520: Interest paid on your student loans (amount from line
31900 of your return) ^

Line 34 and line 58560: Your tuition, education, and textbook amounts (attach
Schedule NU(S11)) ^

Line 35 and line 58600: Tuition, education, and textbook amounts transferred
from a child or grandchild ^

Line 36 and line 58640: Amounts transferred from your spouse or common-law
partner (attach Schedule NU(S2)) ^

Line 37: Add lines 32 to 36. = ^

Medical expenses:
Line 38 and line 58689: Amount from line 33099 of your return ^

Line 39: Amount from line 23600 of your return ^

Line 40: Applicable rate 3%

Line 41: Line 39 multiplied by the percentage from line 40 = ^

Line 42: Enter whichever is less: $2,834 or the amount on line 41. ^

Line 43: Line 38 minus line 42 (if negative, enter "0") = ^

Line 44 and line 58729: Allowable amount of medical expenses for other
dependants from line 33199 of your return ^

Line 45 and line 58769: Line 43 plus line 44 = ^

Line 46 and line 58800: Line 37 plus line 45 = ^

Line 47: Nunavut non-refundable tax credit rate 4%

Line 48 and line 58840: Line 46 multiplied by the percentage from line 47 = ^

Donations and gifts:
Line 49: Amount from line 13 of your federal Schedule 9 ^
multiplied by 4% = ^

Line 50: Amount from line 14 of your federal Schedule 9 ^
multiplied by 11.5% = ^

Line 51 and line 58969: Line 49 plus line 50 = ^

Line 52 and line 61500: Line 48 plus line 51 Enter this amount on line 56.
Nunavut non-refundable tax credits = ^

5014-C (Page 3 of 3)

Part C - Nunavut tax

Line 53: Nunavut tax on taxable income from line 8 ^

Line 54 and line 61510: Nunavut tax on split income (complete Form T1206) ^

Line 55: Line 53 plus line 54 = ^

Line 56: Nunavut non-refundable tax credits from line 52 ^

Line 57 and line 61520: Nunavut dividend tax credit (use Worksheet NU428) ^

Nunavut minimum tax carryover:
Line 58 and line 61540: Amount from line 40427 of your return ^
multiplied by 45% = ^

Line 59: Add lines 56 to 58. = ^

Line 60: Line 55 minus line 59 (if negative, enter "0") = ^

Nunavut additional tax for minimum tax purposes:
Line 61: Amount from line 14 of Part 7 of Form T691 ^
multiplied by 45% = ^

Line 62: Line 60 plus line 61 = ^

Line 63: Territorial foreign tax credit (complete Form T2036) ^

Line 64: Line 62 minus line 63 (if negative, enter "0") = ^

Line 65 and line 62290: Tax credit for volunteer firefighters and search and
rescue volunteers Claim $722 ^

Line 66: Line 64 minus line 65 (if negative, enter "0") Enter this amount on
line 42800 of your return. Nunavut tax = ^

Details of amount for young children(if you need more space, attach an
additional page)

Child's name ^
Relationship to you ^
Date of birth (Year/Month/Day) ^
Social insurance number (if available) (enter 9 digit number) ^

See the privacy notice on your return.
